title: hibiscus
description: Hibiscus api is mainly rendering focused API designed to simplify the awkward parts of Minecraft
body:
PRE - ALPHA - ARTISTS LITERALLY HAVEN'T MADE THE MOD ICON YET
---
### Hibiscus API
A FabricMC 1.21.1 primarily rendering API with many fully modular features.

This mod is an API! This means that by default, it does not add anything!

<details>
  <summary>All current features</summary>
  Simple overveiw of what each "module" does. Visit the Wiki for more information
  
  ---
  <details>
  <summary>Compat</summary>
    Adds a new Java Annotation @RequiresMod. This allows for easy to use compatibility between mods as code will only run when the mod is installed
  </details>
  <details>
  <summary>GUI</summary>
    Implementing `VariableModel` or `VariableTexture` allows you to change model and texture at any point, through the implemented methods
  </details>
  <details>
  <summary>Prefab</summary>
    Not complete. These are item classes that automatically generate their item stats
  </details>
  <details>
  <summary>Rendering</summary>
    World Rendering allows for you to use any of the default rendering classes to quickly create in world render options, using any of the options from `WorldRenderOptions`

These include 

      1. Shape Renderer
      
      2. Chain Renderer
      
      3. Spotlight Beam Renderer
      
      4. Sky Beam Renderer
      
      5. Symbol Renderer
  </details>
  <details>
  <summary>Util</summary>
    Math and Colour helpers.
  </details>
</details>

## Installation
In `build.gradle`, add the Modrinth maven to your dependencies  
```
repositories { 
    maven { url = "https://api.modrinth.com/maven" }
}
```
Then, in the `dependencies` block of `build.gradle` add
```
dependencies {
    modImplementation "maven.modrinth:hibiscus:${project.hibiscus_version}"
}
```
Then add the `hibiscus_version` object in `gradle.properties` corresponding to the Hibiscus version you want to use
